channel.title: Comments for page "Chat Guide"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Chat Guide"

item.title: The Lounge is Down
item.content:encoded: RIP the Lounge

item.title: The Lounge is Down
item.content:encoded: F in the chat for the Lounge…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Under NSFW, what's the difference between "outright porn" and "explicit/sexual images"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm curious, what's the password?

You'll need to be more specific here. If you're asking for a chat password, that's something you create for your own chat nickname (and should NOT be shared with anyone).

I'm trying to make an SCP and one of the links on the guides said to come here for criticism.

You need to follow the instructions on the page to use the chat widget. This discussion thread is not for seeking critique, it's for discussing how to use the IRC chatrooms.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hello? I'm curious, what's the password? I'm trying to make an SCP and one of the links on the guides said to come here for criticism.

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The images on this guide were taken by pxdnbluesoul, who has released them under Public Domain.

item.title: We're missing a channel…
item.content:encoded:
I don't know if this should be asked, but should the #ethicscommittee channel be included here, or is it considered "not friendly enough" to be shown in the main list?

If so, just put it in the list as the channel that's there "because being an author is stressful."

Edit: nvm. It's not official, so it wouldn't be included anyway.

item.title: Re: Channels
item.content:encoded: You can use /list command to get a list of all currently available channels on the server. Source - Basic IRC Commands.

item.title: Re: Channels
item.content:encoded: Erm… there's a lot of channels in the IRC in general. Why do you need a list of all of them?

item.title: Channels
item.content:encoded: Is there a complete list of channels for the irc anywhere. Other than the official team chan I don't know what other ones are there?

item.title: disregard
item.content:encoded: disregard

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is not the place to ask if your SCP draft is any good - this is the discussion page for the Chat Guide, nothing to do with drafts.

If this is your first SCP article, you need to use the Ideas and Brainstorming Forum in order to get two greenlights before asking for feedback on your sandbox, in a process explained here -

http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-10629348/new-draft-forum-policies

Please be more careful in the future regarding where you post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can someone say if my SCP is good or not please http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/mechasaur44

item.title: Re: New on-page IRC chat client doesn’t work on Safari (iPad Pro gen 1, iOS 12,3,1, Wi-Fi)
item.content:encoded: If this occurs you can continue to use the old mibbit client at http://05command.wikidot.com/chat and there is a link for this at the very bottom of the chat guide.

item.title: New on-page IRC chat client doesn’t work on Safari (iPad Pro gen 1, iOS 12,3,1, Wi-Fi)
item.content:encoded:
I have trouble with new version of the on-page IRC chat client.
It did loaded and logged in just fine.
Stats page and NickServ was working
But. After I joined the channel. I can’t type anything.
The typing space was gone. Every time I try to use it on my iPad
Here is a picture. http://scpsandboxcn.wikidot.com/local--files/potato-trolozy/ChatClntBug
I haven’t tried the full-screen version.

for:pxdnbluesoul
Yes, I can use other stuff. (And I did), But this was something needed to be fixed, this was a bug report, Find.more iPad user to test this if available.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty sure? If not, I can always wait the day or two before trying again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Up to a day or two in some cases, I think.

That said, the command to register a chat nick is /msg NickServ register PASSWORD EMAIL with "register". Is that the command you used?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So I did the /msg NickServ [password] [email], but I can't seem to find the email anywhere. I've checked in spam as well. How long does it take for the email to deliver?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did you join the correct #site19 and in the correct server? You need to join via SynIRC and #site19 (note #site-19 or other variants).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey there, so I’ve joined site 19 and registered, when I open it and do /msg NickServ IDENTIFY it says my password has been accepted, but it appears as if nobody is actually in site19. I’m on mobile so that could possibly be the problem but I’m unsure

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: IRC is honestly not that much harder to join than Discord — the process of registering an IRC nick is virtually identical to registering a Discord account. The only difference is that IRC's registry process starts via IRC command-line instead of a graphical user interface. Moreover, Discord is banned by a lot of firewalls, whereas IRC is not. Finally, Discord's extra features like emojis and voice chat are ultimately unnecessary for a functioning community chat, and its moderation features are pathetically useless in comparison to IRC's.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why don't we just use something that seems less confusing, like discord or something akin to that?

item.title: Re: Newbie Help please I don't know how to IRC...
item.content:encoded:
Try accessing #site17 via the staff records site widget: http://05command.wikidot.com/chat

Ask for a chat operator to assist you with registering your nick.

item.title: Newbie Help please I don't know how to IRC...
item.content:encoded:
Hey there,

Never used this kind of chat before and I cannot process what exactly I have to do to join the chat. I've tried putting in the /msg NickServ register thing but nothing seems to work? Am I a complete baby on the internet? Goddamn, please help.

Thanks!

item.title: Re: Site 19 on mobile?
item.content:encoded: It should work fine on mobile. It sounds like you didn't register your nick properly, since attempting to join #site19 without a registered nick results in seeing only an "empty" room (and typically there's upwards of 50 or so people idling in 19 at pretty much any given time).

item.title: Site 19 on mobile?
item.content:encoded: Just wondering, does anyone know if site 19 works on mobile because I'm having some trouble connecting. After connection I end up in a blank server named "#site19(0)" where there aren't any messages and I can't send any. Thanks

item.title: Re: Newbie Help
item.content:encoded: You may need to re-register your nick. #site19 is pretty much never dead; usually there's at least a few people idling.

item.title: Re: Newbie Help
item.content:encoded: I think the chat is dead. Although, I've been away for awhile, so maybe it's still there, and I'm connecting to the wrong thing

item.title: Newbie Help
item.content:encoded:
I can't tell if the chat is broken, or if I am just not able access it properly.
I type in a name, press the button, and the chat comes up, but I can't see any posts, and typing does nothing.
Is there an explanation for this?

item.title: Re: Dunno why
item.content:encoded:
oh shit lol, thats 2012.
my bad bruv

item.title: Re: Dunno why
item.content:encoded:
/join #site19

it says that in the article above the chat room, read it

item.title: Re: Need Help
item.content:encoded:
This question should be posted to the Questions Desk forum: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/c-891087/help:questions-desk, not the chat guide discussion.

For the answer to your question, please read through the "posting" section of the How to Write an SCP Guide.

item.title: Need Help
item.content:encoded:
I just posted an scp of mine (3948) and the page is there, but the name of it is still [ACCESS DENIED]

can someone help me out?

item.title: Re: Having trouble connecting
item.content:encoded:
I only have one question that I would like an answer for: Is SCP-087-B an actual SCP or just some other thing that someone has made up and given a SCP designation because I cannot find SCP-087-B in the search bar?

This is something to ask in the main forums, not the discussion page for the Chat Guide. Try the Foundation Universe subforum: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/c-88827/foundation-universe

item.title: Having trouble connecting
item.content:encoded:
Having major problems connecting to the site19 server to the point where I cannot ask questions.

I only have one question that I would like an answer for: Is SCP-087-B an actual SCP or just some other thing that someone has made up and given a SCP designation because I cannot find SCP-087-B in the search bar?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The mibbet widget seems to work alright for me, so it may be a local issue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This keeps happening to me as well. Though it may just be a bandwidth/some other issue on my part. :/

EDIT: Chat module just loaded. Took a while though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Chat module is 404'ing for some reason. Same thing is happening on 05command. Either Mibbit is finally kill or my phone is just rejecting it due to how old it is.

6/22 update: Chat module still broken and IRCCloud hasn't worked in days. I'm thinking that syncIRC might be having issues cause I've seen reports of other IRC clients acting funny. just in time for the 4000 contest

item.title: Re: Um
item.content:encoded: Is giving the 'cannot connect to widget02.mibbet.com' error. Standard sort of thing. Because all the other mibbet pages work I thought that maybe they had updated something or changed a url that made the embed code not work, but if it works for you then its definitely local.

item.title: Re: Um
item.content:encoded: Might be a bug, but it seems to be displaying okay for me. Can you describe the error in a bit more detail? Is it just not showing up at all? If that's the case, it may be that you need to enable javascript or cookies for the chat guide page.

item.title: Um
item.content:encoded:
Is the chat guide IRC module not working for anyone else?

I assumed it was a local issue, but every other page including mibbit's own seems to work fine.

item.title: Re: Help!
item.content:encoded: You need to identify each time to join- type /msg nickserv identify password

item.title: Re: Help!
item.content:encoded:
You need to log in to the username.
When you join IRC type /msg nickserv identify password

Replace password with your password then it should say you've registered the nick. After this you can type /join #site19

item.title: Help!
item.content:encoded: I tried registering a nickname, went to my email, clicked the link to prove its me, but when I try joining site 19, it still says I don’t have a registered nick!! (I’m using iOS btw)

item.title: Re: Problem
item.content:encoded:
aHA! Thanks!
But how do I add a nickname again?

item.title: Re: Problem
item.content:encoded:
Might make sure you're connected to the irc.synirc.net server (as opposed to, say, irc.freenode.net or irc.quakenet.org)-
there are a number of different irc servers and they don't share channels between them

item.title: Problem
item.content:encoded:
When I typed /join #site19 IRCCloud (iOS), it added me to a seemingly empty channel.
I did all of the nickname stuff too

item.title: Staff post - closed, do not respond to this post
item.content:encoded: Apocolyps, as per the post title, do not respond to closed staff posts.

item.title: Re: Staff post - closed, do not respond to this post
item.content:encoded: Thanks

item.title: Staff post - closed, do not respond to this post
item.content:encoded:
That's not how this works. You want idea feedback, you go to the Ideas and Brainstorming subforum. Click this text for it.

Also, chat probably "isn't working for you" because you haven't registered your nick. If you need help with that, you want the chat over //here// - which doesn't need a registered nick.

item.title: Chat won't work for me
item.content:encoded:
So I will put this here:

Ideas for SCPs(I have not written any of them yet and this would be my first SCP)

A creature that absorbs the anomalous properties of any anomalous object it touches
A tablet that can answer any question for a price related to the question
“The King of the White Void” which can create anomalous objects/creatures depending on the environment around it
A joke scp that is the result of James observing the “Broken God” file

Please inform me if any of these are already done or are hard to do ideas

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Probably worthy to note Chatzilla's no longer compatible with Firefox. That's at least what it says on the add-on page.

e: Specifically the new Quantum version. I assume it's compatible with the old but as that's no longer the standard - eh.

item.title: Re: Email Issues
item.content:encoded:
Is there a timer on the nick? Maybe if I wait a few days and try again with a different email, it will reset.

I believe so. Alternatively, there was a delay. If you still encounter this issue, try hopping into #site17 (which doesn't require a registered nick) and asking one of our chat operators to help you out.

item.title: Re: Email Issues
item.content:encoded:
When I used /msg NickServ register, everything worked fine. There were no issues when I initially typed in the command. The notification had told me to confirm my email or something along those lines.
Is there a timer on the nick? Maybe if I wait a few days and try again with a different email, it will reset.

item.title: Re: Email Issues
item.content:encoded: Are you getting some sort of notification from NickServ (in the chat window) after you submit the command to register a nick? If so, what's the text on that notification?

item.title: Email Issues
item.content:encoded:
I am so so sorry if this has come up before, or if I'm just stupid.

I am not receiving the confirmation code whilst trying to register my nick.
I've tried resending it.
I've tried resending it again.
I thoroughly checked to make sure I didn't make a spelling error, refreshed the spam folder, and tried canceling the registration but to no avail. Maybe I'm missing something? Again, I'm so sorry if this has come up before.

item.title: Re: Update?
item.content:encoded:
I have a question: Why doesn't this website have a dedicated Discord server rather than all of the IRC?

  • http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-3768662 (this thread in particular has good information)
  • http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-2096209
  • http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-1371123
  • http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-3313142

They seem really clunky for mobile users, I can't stand being in chat when it's this clunky (Especially compared to Discord)

I personally don't chat with my mobile device; I'm inclined to believe I'm not the only person like that. Additionally, there isn't anything Discord offers that would be an improvement over IRC without being a ton of extra work for staff (who, by the way, don't get paid at all for the work they do on the site).

item.title: Re: Update?
item.content:encoded:
I suppose I'll have to do that then.
Thanks for respousing uwu

item.title: Re: Update?
item.content:encoded: Because the number of people who use IRC and want to stay with it outweighs the number of people who want to switch everything to discord. If this page is too 'clunky' for you on mobile, maybe try an app like IRCCloud?

item.title: Update?
item.content:encoded: I have a question: Why doesn't this website have a dedicated Discord server rather than all of the IRC? They seem really clunky for mobile users, I can't stand being in chat when it's this clunky (Especially compared to Discord)
